parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 4 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Segment Audience

### Segment Audience

Two-way gate on `={{ ($json.score ?? 50) >= 50 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Step 1 — Welcome (Telegram) · ②→ Tag for Slow Nurture

### Step 1 — Welcome (Telegram)

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Next: Wait Before Step 2

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Tag for Slow Nurture

Upserts a row into 'Lead Tracker', matched on `leadId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Wait Before Step 2

Pauses 2 days — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Converted Already?

### Converted Already?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.status }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Step 2 — Value Nudge (Telegram) · ②→ Mark Converted — Stop Drip

### Step 2 — Value Nudge (Telegram)

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Next: Wait Before Final Step

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Mark Converted — Stop Drip

Upserts a row into 'Lead Tracker', matched on `leadId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Wait Before Final Step

Pauses 3 days — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Step 3 — Final Offer (Telegram)

### Step 3 — Final Offer (Telegram)

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Next: Update Drip Status

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Update Drip Status

Upserts a row into 'Lead Tracker', matched on `leadId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Next: Send Summary to Telegram

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Telegram

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Telegram bot token

# Nurture trial users with Google Sheets and Telegram

**Pattern:** Drip Sequence · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Nurture trial users with Google Sheets and Telegram. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual enrichment is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Segment Audience → Step 1 — Welcome (Telegram) → Wait Before Step 2 → Converted Already? → Step 2 — Value Nudge (Telegram) → Wait Before Final Step → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to drip sequence without manual effort
- Anyone automating enrichment in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Improves response time
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Every step checks 'Converted Already?' before sending — a drip campaign that keeps emailing someone who already bought is the fastest way to burn trust. The Wait node's durable resume state (via `webhookId`) is what lets a multi-day wait survive an n8n restart instead of being held only in memory.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a 4th drip step with its own wait/exit-check pair.
2. Manually flip a test record's status to 'converted' mid-drip and confirm the ladder stops sending.
3. Shorten the wait durations so the full ladder runs in minutes instead of days, for testing.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your enrichment process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
